description: The existing test finalize_multisig_and_extract_finalizes_buyer_input_too (psbt_tests.rs:398) bypasses the sale template by passing seller_sale_sig_hex: None and signing the final tx directly. The actual production flow (build_locking_psbt -> sign template -> extract_seller_sale_sig -> build_protected_sale_psbt with embedded sig -> apply_marketplace_signature -> finalize_multisig_and_extract) has zero test coverage. Add an e2e test that exercises the full chain and verifies the final tx.

description:
Create new API endpoint to serve bitmap details with data from DB + Ordinal API.

Requirements:
- Add route GET /bitmap/:block_height/details in src/routes/bitmaps.rs (new file)
- Query DB: get bitmap by block_height → returns inscription_id, traits[]
- Call OrdClient.get_inscription(inscription_id) to get:
  - owner (from address field)
  - children (count them)
  - genesis_height
- Return JSON:
  {
    block_height: i64,
    inscription_id: String,
    inscription_number: i64,
    owner: String,
    traits: Vec<String>,
    children_count: usize,
    genesis_height: i64
  }

Files to modify:
- src/routes/mod.rs - add new router
- src/routes/bitmaps.rs - new file with endpoint
- src/db/bitmaps.rs - add get_bitmap_by_height query

Note: OrdClient already exists at src/services/ord.rs with get_inscription() method.

While there are no passwords to brute-force, someone could spam profile creation filling up the profiles table. Fix: Add a stricter per-IP rate limit on /api/auth/* routes (e.g., 3 req/s, 10 burst) or add a cooldown between profile creations from the same IP.

close_reason: Fixed: threaded seller_address and price_sats through LockingPsbtRequest -> build_locking_psbt -> build_sale_template_psbt so the sale template output[0] matches the final sale tx. Added test coverage.
description: build_sale_template_psbt (psbt.rs:732-735) uses placeholder output[0] = {locked_value, empty_script} but the final sale tx in build_protected_sale_psbt has output[0] = {price_sats, seller_address}. Since SIGHASH_SINGLE|ANYONECANPAY commits to output[0], the sighash differs and the seller pre-signature is invalid. Fix: thread seller_address and price_sats through LockingPsbtRequest -> build_locking_psbt -> build_sale_template_psbt so the template uses the real committed output.
title: CRITICAL: Sale template output mismatch invalidates seller pre-signature

All 56 tests pass.
description: Align bitmap-marketplace with mempool-protection reference by replacing P2WSH CHECKMULTISIG with Taproot script-path using CHECKSIG+CHECKSIGADD. Excludes marketplace fee logic (already diverged intentionally). Covers: multisig script construction, address generation, signing (ECDSA→Schnorr), finalization (witness→tapScriptSig+controlBlock), sale template, and all tests.
title: Migrate 2-of-2 multisig from P2WSH to Taproot script-path (P2TR)

description:
Define the actual block height lists for filter categories that can't be computed deterministically.

These will be used by the /explore/blocks endpoint. Store as static arrays or load from DB/config.

## Categories needing curation
- patoshi: blocks mined by Satoshi (patoshi pattern) — known block ranges, e.g. ~0-18000 with specific coinbase patterns
- pizza: Bitcoin pizza day block and surrounding notable blocks (block 57,043 area)
- epic-sat: blocks containing epic-rarity satoshis (halving blocks: 210000, 420000, 630000, 840000)
- billionaire: blocks where tx_count >= 1000 (or community-defined list)
- pristine-punk: early punk blocks (height % 4 == 3 AND height < 1000)
- perfect-punk: palindrome blocks that would also be punk type (palindrome AND height % 4 == 3)

Note: pristine-punk and perfect-punk can actually be computed — implement as deterministic filters.

## Deliverable
Replace stub arrays in explore.rs with proper definitions for each category.

Depends on: bitmap-marketplace-vs2
title: Seed curated block lists for patoshi, pizza, pristine-punk, perfect-punk filter categories

description: Add to src/services/psbt.rs: build_locking_psbt() (P2WSH 2-of-2, BIP-67 key sort, dynamic fee, gas funding input if <343 sats), build_protected_sale_psbt() (multisig UTXO with SINGLE|ACP for input 0), apply_marketplace_signature() (SIGHASH_ALL co-sig), finalize_multisig_input() (P2WSH witness [OP_0, seller_sig, marketplace_sig, redeem_script]). Unit tests for each.
title: Implement locking and protected sale PSBT builder functions

description:
Add a new Axum route that returns paginated block heights for a given filter category. Used by the frontend explore page to support collection-based filtering with prev/next navigation.

## Endpoint
GET /explore/blocks?filter=<category>&page=0&limit=9

## Query params
- filter: string (required) — one of: punks, palindrome, sub-100k, nakamoto, patoshi, billionaire, epic-sat, pizza, pristine-punk, perfect-punk
- page: u64 (default 0)
- limit: u64 (default 9, max 50)

## Response JSON
{
  heights: [u64],      // block heights for this page
  has_more: bool,
  has_prev: bool,      // page > 0
  total: Option<u64>   // if deterministic/known count
}

## Filter logic

### Deterministic (pure computation, no DB needed)
- punks: heights where height % 4 == 3
- palindrome: heights whose decimal string is a palindrome
- sub-100k: heights 0..99_999
- nakamoto: heights 0..36_288 (approximate Satoshi-era)

### Curated (return hardcoded representative list for now, wire real data later)
- patoshi, billionaire, epic-sat, pizza, pristine-punk, perfect-punk: return stub data / curated arrays

## Files to create/modify
- src/routes/explore.rs (new) — handler + filter logic
- src/routes/mod.rs — register /explore/blocks route
title: Add GET /explore/blocks endpoint for filtered block height pagination

description: Migration: ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMNS seller_pubkey TEXT, multisig_address TEXT, multisig_script TEXT, locking_raw_tx TEXT, protection_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'none' CHECK IN ('none','locking_pending','active'). ALTER TABLE sales ADD COLUMNS sale_psbt TEXT, locking_tx_id TEXT. Update Listing model struct.
title: Add mempool protection columns to listings and sales tables

description: Delete the stub make_offer fn (lines 143-148) and remove /offer from the orders router. Real implementation lives at POST /offers.
title: Remove make_offer stub from orders.rs

description: Comparative review found critical gaps between bitmap-marketplace Rust PSBT implementation and the battle-tested mempool-protection TS library. Four actionable issues: missing seller pre-signature, dynamic locking fee, marketplace fee output, UTXO liveness check.
title: PSBT mempool protection: align with battle-tested reference

description: finalize_multisig_and_extract expects both seller and marketplace partial_sigs on input 0, but seller never signs the sale PSBT. Fix: generate sale template at listing time, have seller sign with SIGHASH_SINGLE|ANYONECANPAY, store partial_sig in DB, embed into sale PSBT at buy time.
title: Add seller pre-signature on sale template at listing time

description: DEFAULT_LOCKING_TX_FEE_SATS=13 only covers single p2wpkh input at 0.1 sat/vB. The gas validation already calculates correct dynamic fee but locked_value_sats ignores it. Fix: use ceil(vbytes * relay_fee_rate).
title: Replace flat 13-sat locking fee with dynamic calculation

description: Neither build_protected_sale_psbt nor build_buy_psbt adds a marketplace fee output. Fee model: max(BPS% of price, 1000 sats), buyer pays on top, sent to MARKETPLACE_FEE_ADDRESS env var. Also delete dead royalty code (royalty.rs, royalty_address/royalty_bps on listings), replace royalty_sats with marketplace_fee_sats in sales table.
title: Add marketplace fee output to buy PSBTs and remove dead royalty code

description: confirm_order and accept_offer broadcast package without checking input UTXOs are still unspent. Fix: call gettxout for locking tx inputs and buyer funding input before submitpackage, return clear error messages.
title: Add pre-broadcast UTXO liveness check before submitpackage
